question: What is lazy evaluation in Spark and what is the difference between transformations and actions?
answer:
Lazy evaluation means Spark builds an execution plan but does NOT compute anything until an action is called.

Transformations (lazy, return a new DataFrame/RDD):
  df.select(), .filter(), .withColumn(), .groupBy(), .join(), .distinct()
  df.map(), .flatMap(), .union(), .repartition()
- Spark records these as a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) of operations
- No data is read or computed

Actions (trigger computation, return results or write data):
  df.count()       # returns Int
  df.collect()     # returns list of Rows to driver (dangerous on large data)
  df.show(20)      # prints rows
  df.write.parquet(...)
  df.take(10)      # returns first N rows
  df.first()       # returns first Row
  df.toPandas()    # collects all data to driver as pandas DataFrame

Why it matters:
1. You can chain many transformations cheaply; only pay compute on action
2. Catalyst optimizer sees the full plan before executing
3. Calling count() twice runs the job twice (no automatic caching)
4. Use cache()/persist() before multiple actions on the same DataFrame
5. Each action triggers a new Spark job; minimize actions in loops
